name: Palace Saimin
tagline: Hand-wrapped won tons since 1946 — if this place closes, Honolulu loses its soul.
mustOrder: won ton min ($9–12, hand-wrapped won tons in pork-shrimp broth)
teriyaki beef sticks ($2–4 each — NON-NEGOTIABLE side order)
parking: Small lot next to the building on King Street
talkStory: Family-run since 1946. Saimin is Hawaii's own noodle soup — evolved on sugar plantations where Japanese, Chinese, Filipino, and Portuguese workers shared ingredients.
insiderTips: Combine with Helena's (also in Kalihi) for a full local food pilgrimage.
